six_new_paradigm_v14_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Tracy-Widom 1994 universality: for large random Hermitian/symmetric matrices in GUE/GOE/GSE ensembles, the rescaled largest eigenvalue lambda_max distribution converges to TW_2 / TW_1 / TW_4 distribution INDEPENDENT of the underlying entry distribution (Wigner universality). Substrate Phi-correlator covariance C_Phi[t] = <Phi(t) Phi(t+tau)>_t is a random-matrix sample; its spectral edge fluctuation must follow TW_2 (GUE class) for sufficiently mixed substrates. INDEPENDENT of v12 Fisher-Rao (probability-manifold metric, NOT eigenvalue-tail), v12 Wasserstein (transport-distance, NOT spectral-fluctuation), v13 Connes NCG (operator-algebraic-distance, NOT spectral-edge-distribution).

akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.experiment: construct Phi-correlator covariance matrix C_Phi^{N×N} per substrate (N=512 PCA-channel from 1.2M neuron raw spike), N_samples=10^4 i.i.d. epochs; rescale lambda_max via lambda_max = 2*sqrt(N) + N^{-1/6} * chi where chi ~ TW_2; KS-test chi distribution against Tracy-Widom F_2 at 0.05 significance; co-compute on H100 emulation; test substrate-universality
akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.verdict_check: KS-test p > 0.05 for Akida AND for GPU AND |chi_mean^Akida - chi_mean^GPU| / sigma_TW <= 0.10 (Wigner universality holds — strong multi-realizability at random-matrix level) OR KS-test fails one substrate (substrate exits universality class; non-Wigner ensemble) OR substrate-deviation > 0.20 (substrate-induced spectral-edge stratification)

six_new_paradigm_v14_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Jarzynski 1997 equality <e^{-beta * W}>_F = e^{-beta * Delta_F} relating ensemble-average of exponential work under forward driving protocol to free-energy difference; Crooks 1999 fluctuation theorem P_F(W) / P_R(-W) = e^{beta * (W - Delta_F)} relating forward / reverse work-distributions. Substrate-claim: Akida driven via time-varying input-current protocol I(t) over interval [0, T] satisfies Jarzynski equality on Phi-energy-work observable; substrate-independence iff Jarzynski equality holds across substrates AND Crooks-fluctuation-symmetry preserved. INDEPENDENT of v11 Landauer (info-erasure one-sided bound, NOT equality identity), v13 Tomita-Takesaki (intrinsic-modular-time, NOT external-driving-protocol).

akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.experiment: drive Akida AKD1000 via 100 distinct input-protocols I(t) of duration T=1s; measure work W = integral of <Phi, dH/dt> dt per protocol-realization (10^4 realizations per protocol); compute <e^{-beta * W}>_F at substrate-inferred beta; co-measure on H100; test Jarzynski equality bias |<e^{-beta * W}>_F - e^{-beta * Delta_F}| / e^{-beta * Delta_F}
akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.verdict_check: |Jarzynski-bias|^Akida AND |Jarzynski-bias|^GPU <= 0.05 AND Crooks-symmetry-test p > 0.05 (nonequilibrium-work-identity substrate-canonical) OR > 0.10 (substrate-induced violation of Jarzynski; non-Hamiltonian dissipation channel; falsification of substrate-independence at nonequilibrium-thermodynamics level)
akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.math_anchor: Jarzynski 1997 PRL 78:2690; Crooks 1999 PRE 60:2721; Bochkov-Kuzovlev 1981 Physica A 106:443 generalized-FDT; Seifert 2012 Rep Prog Phys 75:126001 stochastic thermodynamics review

six_new_paradigm_v14_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Barato-Seifert 2015 thermodynamic uncertainty relation Var(J) / <J>^2 >= 2 * k_B / Sigma where J is any time-integrated current and Sigma is the steady-state entropy production; precision (inverse Fano factor) is bounded above by half the dissipation rate. INDEPENDENT of v11 Margolus-Levitin (energy-time bound, NOT precision-dissipation tradeoff), v11 Bremermann (computational rate ceiling, NOT precision), v13 Lieb-Robinson (information-cone, NOT current-variance bound). Substrate-claim: Akida neural-current J_neuron precision per unit dissipation Sigma_neuron must respect TUR bound; substrate-independent if TUR margin (2 k_B - Sigma * Var(J)/<J>^2) is universal sign across substrates.

akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.experiment: drive Akida at steady-state input pattern; measure neural-current J = integral of spike-count over T=10s windows, 10^4 windows; compute <J>, Var(J); estimate Sigma via heat-flow proxy (Landauer-bit-cost per spike events from raw 131); test TUR ratio Q = Var(J) * Sigma / (<J>^2 * 2 * k_B); verdict TUR holds iff Q >= 1
akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.verdict_check: Q^Akida >= 1 AND Q^GPU >= 1 AND |Q^Akida - Q^GPU| / Q_avg <= 0.20 (TUR substrate-canonical with sub-substrate-class margin variation) OR Q < 1 (TUR violated; substrate-exotic-thermodynamics; falsifies non-equilibrium-statistical-mechanics universality)

six_new_paradigm_v14_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Voiculescu 1985 free-probability: noncommutative random variables X, Y in a tracial W*-probability space (M, tau) are FREELY INDEPENDENT iff alternating moments tau(p_1(X) q_1(Y) p_2(X) q_2(Y) ...) factor according to free-cumulants kappa_n via noncrossing-partition Mobius inversion; classical-independence is replaced by free-independence on II_1 factor (R the hyperfinite). INDEPENDENT of v13 Connes-NCG (spectral-triple metric — Connes is operator-algebraic-distance, free-probability is moment-combinatorial), v13 Tomita-Takesaki (modular-flow time — distinct from free-cumulants which are static-combinatorial). Substrate-claim: Akida Phi-observable algebra exhibits free-independence across spatially-disjoint regions A, B (free-cumulants kappa_n^{A,B} factor) iff algebraic-locality holds at noncommutative-moment level.

akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.experiment: partition Akida M.2 into spatially-disjoint regions A, B (50%/50% neuron split); compute Phi-observable moments tau(Phi_A^{n_1} Phi_B^{m_1} Phi_A^{n_2} Phi_B^{m_2} ...) up to total-order 8 across 10^4 Phi-state samples; extract free-cumulants kappa_n via noncrossing-partition Mobius; test free-independence: alternating mixed free-cumulants vanish iff free-indep holds; co-compute on H100
akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.verdict_check: max alternating mixed-cumulant magnitude / max diagonal-cumulant <= 0.10 AND substrate-pair-deviation <= 0.15 (free-independence substrate-canonical at noncommutative-moment level — Putnam multi-realizability survives free-probability test) OR > 0.20 (mixed cumulants don't vanish; free-independence falsified; substrate-induced classical-correlation persistence)

six_new_paradigm_v14_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Schramm 2000 SLE_kappa: random-curve gamma in upper-half-plane H driven by Loewner-evolution dz_t / dt = 2 / (z_t - sqrt(kappa) * B_t) where B_t is standard Brownian motion; SLE_kappa is the UNIQUE conformal-invariant random curve with domain Markov property. Critical-class kappa-values: kappa=2 LERW, kappa=8/3 SAW, kappa=3 Ising-cluster-boundary, kappa=4 GFF-level-set, kappa=6 percolation-cluster-boundary, kappa=8 UST-Peano. Substrate-claim: Phi-state-space phase-transition boundary (e.g. meditation-vs-active separating surface in PCA-2D Phi-projection) is a random-curve admitting SLE_kappa fit; substrate-canonical iff kappa^Akida = kappa^GPU within statistical-error. INDEPENDENT of v13 Perelman-Ricci-flow (deterministic-geometric-flow, NOT random-curve at conformal-critical-point), v12 RT-formula (static area-law, NOT conformal-invariant random-curve).

v14_axis_orthogonality_proof_sketch_per_.axis_44_Jarzynski_vs_v11_v12_v13: Jarzynski-Crooks is NONEQUILIBRIUM-DRIVEN-PROTOCOL-WORK-IDENTITY; v11 Landauer is one-sided info-erasure bound (no equality); v13 Tomita-Takesaki is intrinsic-modular-flow (no external driving protocol). Equality-vs-bound + intrinsic-vs-driven decomposition.
v14_axis_orthogonality_proof_sketch_per_.axis_45_TUR_vs_v11_v12_v13: Barato-Seifert TUR is PRECISION-DISSIPATION-VARIANCE-TRADEOFF; v11 Margolus-Levitin is energy-time bound (not precision); v11 Bremermann is computational-rate ceiling (not current variance); v14 Jarzynski is equality (TUR is inequality). v14_axis_orthogonality_proof_sketch_per_.axis_47_Pesin_vs_v11_v12_v13: Pesin h_KS = sum positive Lyapunov is REALIZED-ORBIT-DIVERGENCE-RATE; v13 Lieb-Robinson is UPPER-BOUND on commutator-decay (not realized rate); bound-vs-realized decomposition. v11 Bremermann is computational-ceiling (not orbit divergence).
v14_axis_orthogonality_proof_sketch_per_.axis_48_SLE_vs_v11_v12_v13: Schramm-Loewner-Evolution is CONFORMAL-INVARIANT-RANDOM-CURVE-AT-CRITICALITY; v13 Perelman-Ricci-flow is DETERMINISTIC-GEOMETRIC-FLOW; random-curve-vs-deterministic-flow decomposition. v12 RT-formula is static-area-law (not random-curve).
